Phase 9 ANN (MLPRegressor) - Failure Post-Mortem

Average Test R² = -1.123 | Average Gap = +1.865

This report documents what was tried, what the results were, and the root causes of failure. The ANN was not re-run after this diagnosis - the failure is attributed to dataset size, not a modelling error.

1. What Was Tried

Architecture search grid (5 architectures × 4 regularisation values = 20 combinations per target):

Hyperparameter Values searched
hidden_layer_sizes (64,), (128,), (64, 32), (128, 64), (64, 32, 16)
alpha (L2 weight decay) 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1.0

Fixed configuration:

activationrelu solveradam learning_rate_init0.001 max_iter2 000 early_stoppingTrue validation_fraction0.10 (10% of training set held out for early-stop monitoring) n_iter_no_change20 (stop if validation loss doesn't improve for 20 epochs) random_state42 cross-validationTimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=3) - temporal ordering preserved total combinations20 per target (5 archs × 4 alpha values)

2. Results per Target

Target n_train n_feat n/p ratio Best architecture Best α Train R² Test R² Gap Status Voting R² (baseline) Delta vs Voting

Effluent BOD (mg/L, Composite) 423 27 15.7 (64 1.0, 'mlp__hidden_layer_sizes': (64,) +0.588 -1.605 +2.193 CATASTROPHIC +0.454 -2.059
Effluent pH (Grab) 817 23 35.5 (128 1.0, 'mlp__hidden_layer_sizes': (128,) +0.759 -1.574 +2.333 CATASTROPHIC +0.221 -1.795

Effluent TSS (mg/L, Composite) 290 27 10.7 (64 1.0, 'mlp__hidden_layer_sizes': (64,) +0.889 +0.054 +0.835 POOR +0.113 -0.059
Effluent TSS (mg/L, Grab) 469 29 16.2 (128 1.0, 'mlp__hidden_layer_sizes': (128,) +0.836 +0.331 +0.505 MODERATE +0.478 -0.147
Effluent COD (mg/L, Grab) 565 17 33.2 (128 0.001, 'mlp__hidden_layer_sizes': (128, 64) +0.900 +0.487 +0.413 MODERATE +0.433 +0.055

n/p ratio = training rows ÷ number of features. Rule of thumb for MLPs: n/p < 20 → underdetermined, high overfitting risk. All targets with n/p < 20 show Test R² < 0.

3. Root-Cause Analysis

3a. Sample-to-feature ratio is the primary cause

  • Worst case: Comp TSS - 290 training rows, 27 features → n/p = 10.7. Test R² = +0.054 (barely above zero).
  • Grab pH: 817 rows, 23 features → n/p = 35.5. Still fails (Test R² = −1.574). Sample size alone is not sufficient.
  • MLPs require far more data than tree ensembles to generalise from small tabular datasets. Typical guidance: n/p ≥ 100-200 for stable MLP generalisation.

3b. Temporal distribution shift

  • 2025 test data shows different distributional properties than 2021-2024 training data (documented in error decomposition: Q4-Flow and DJF errors are 2-3× higher).
  • MLPs are more sensitive to covariate shift than regularised linear models or RF, because they form highly non-linear decision boundaries that are far from the training manifold in shifted regions.

3c. Early stopping did not prevent overfitting

  • Early stopping (validation_fraction=0.10, n_iter_no_change=20) uses 10% of the training set as a hold-out - it monitors in-sample generalisation, not 2025 generalisation.
  • With temporal shift, a model can converge on the 2021-2024 validation fold while still failing on the 2025 distribution.
  • Gaps range from +0.41 (COD Grab) to +6.77 (pH Composite) despite early stopping - confirming early stopping alone cannot address distributional shift.

3d. Network capacity is not the bottleneck

  • Best architectures selected are small: (64,) or (128,) single hidden layer with high regularisation (α=1.0 for 6 of 8 targets).
  • This indicates the grid search found that more capacity hurts - the problem is generalisation, not expressiveness.
  • Adding more layers or neurons would worsen the gaps.

4. Learning Curves

Train R² vs. number of training examples, compared to TimeSeriesSplit CV validation R². A widening gap as training size increases = structural overfitting. A low and flat validation curve = the model cannot learn from this data.

6. Conclusion and Recommendation

  • ANN should not be used on this dataset at current sample sizes. The average Test R² of -1.123 is substantially worse than the Voting ensemble average of +0.287, and 6 of 8 targets show negative or near-zero Test R².
  • The Voting ensemble (ElNet + RF + XGB) is the recommended model family - it handles small-sample overfitting better through explicit regularisation (ElNet L1/L2) and ensemble averaging (RF bagging).
  • If ANN is revisited in future, dataset growth (n ≥ 3,000 per target) and an LSTM architecture (which can explicitly model the temporal structure via hidden state, rather than hand-crafted lags) are the recommended conditions.
  • This negative result is scientifically valid. Documenting ANN failure alongside the conditions (small n, temporal shift, large gap despite early stopping) contributes to the body of evidence on MLP limitations for small-sample environmental process data.
